title: A comprehensive guide to the Hexplore It game series
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oKTvQ7d9gYw

Daniel (@dungeondive) delivers an extensive analysis of the entire Hexplore It series, covering all four volumes with detailed pros and cons for each entry. He ranks Valley of the Dead King as his favorite for its simplicity and directness, followed by Domain of Mirza Noctis for its dungeon integration and horror theme, then Force of Adromon for its engaging sandbox feel. His critique of Sands of Shurax—citing excessive complexity, four competing win conditions, and tedious fiddly systems—explains why it's his only ownership regret. Daniel addresses common criticisms including the name, lack of art (mitigated through illustrated decks), gameplay length, and fiddly rules. He emphasizes that despite complexity, these games deliver on the promise of true hex crawl exploration where emergent narrative emerges from connecting random encounters, making them ideal for solo RPG-style play.

----

Which Hexplore It volume would be your entry point into the series, and why?
